title: Antimycobacterial thiobenzanilides
abstract: A group of 30 thiobenzanilides active against Mycobacterium kansasii have been synthesized and their 1 H NMR and UV spectra and R M values (TLC on silica gel impregnated with methylsilicone oil) have been measured. From the correlation between the chemical shifts of the thioamide proton in the 1 H NMR spectra and the Hammett constants it can be concluded that the substituents in both aromatic rings uniformly affect the electron density of the thioamide group. The antimycobacterial activity is probably connected with local molecular parameters and can be considered to be approximately additive with respect to both parts of the molecule.
